briefdescription.para: A function to compare two ClockIdentity values.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the device
is the first ClockIdentity to be compared
is the second ClockIdentity to be compared

simplesect.para: 1 if the two values are equal, 0 if not equal

inbodydescription.para: values are equal

briefdescription.para: A function to compare two PortIdentity values.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the device
is the first sourcePortIdentity to be compared
is the second sourcePortIdentity to be compared

simplesect.para: 1 if the two values are equal, 0 if not equal

inbodydescription.para: values are equal

memberdef.argsstring: (u32 BaseAddress, u32 PtpFrameBaseAddr, u32 PortIdOffset, XAvb_PortIdentity *portID)
memberdef.name: XAvb_GetPortIdentity

briefdescription.para: A function to extract portIdentity information from a received PTP frame.

detaileddescription.para: This can be any portIdentity field (header portIdentity, requestingPortIdentity, etc.)

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the device
is the base address of the received Announce Packet in the Rx PTP Packet Buffer
is the packet offset of the first byte of the portIdentity field to be parsed
is the XAvb_PortIdentity struct that the data will be written to.

simplesect.para: None, but portID will be updated with the portIdentity information

memberdef.argsstring: (u32 BaseAddress, u32 PtpFieldAddress, u32 Data, u32 DataBitEnable, u8 BufferEnable)
memberdef.name: XAvb_WriteToMultipleTxPtpFrames

briefdescription.para: A function to write common data (eg the Source Address) to all PTP frames stored in the Tx PTP Packet buffer.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the device
is the offset address of the relevant field in PTP frames.
is the common data to be written to all Tx PTP frame templates
allows only selected bits of the 32-bit Data word to be modified.
allows the selected buffer to be selected: there are 8 PTP buffers - these are encoded as one-hot. For example, 0x3F will write the selected data to the first 6 buffers only.

simplesect.para: None. But the Tx PTP Packet Buffer is written to as requested

inbodydescription.para: Write to all 8 PTP frame templates
Only write to selected buffers
Read the current value
Only change the selected data bits
Write the updated value

briefdescription.para: This function switches the bytes in a 4-byte word, swapping the MSB for the LSB, and vice-versa.

parameterdescription.para: is the 4-byte input data word

simplesect.para: The input data word with the bytes swapped (most significant down to least significant

briefdescription.para: A function to increment the sequenceId in a PTP frame template.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the device
is the base address of the TX PTP Buffer whose SequenceID is to be incremented

simplesect.para: None. But the relevant TX PTP Packet Buffer is written to with the updated SequenceID

inbodydescription.para: Read the 32-bit BufferWord containing the SequenceId from the PTP buffer
Swap byte order into correct binary and increment the SequenceId
Swap back the byte order into frame storage order
Write the 32-bit BufferWord variable containing the updated SequenceId

briefdescription.para: The software drivers are kept simple by only requesting a single PTP frame to be transmitted at a time.

detaileddescription.para: This function checks (and if necessary waits) until the previously request PTP frame has been transmitted.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAvb instance to be worked on

inbodydescription.para: Wait until any queued PTP frame has been transmitted. This is a software safety feature, not a hardware restriction

briefdescription.para: A function to format then request the transmission of a PTP Announce Packet.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAvb instance to be worked on

simplesect.para: None. But the relevant Tx PTP Packet Buffer is written to with the updated PTP fields, and then the Tx PTP Packet Buffer Control Register is written to request the frame transmission.

inbodydescription.para: Wait until there are no PTP frames to be transmitted
Increment the sequenceId
Send the Announce Frame!

briefdescription.para: A function to format then request the transmission of a PTP Sync Packet.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAvb instance to be worked on

simplesect.para: None. But the relevant Tx PTP Packet Buffer is written to with the updated PTP fields, and then the Tx PTP Packet Buffer Control Register is written to request the frame transmission.

inbodydescription.para: Wait until there are no PTP frames to be transmitted
Increment the sequenceId in the Sync frame
Read the current RTC Offset values
Send the Sync Frame!

para.heading: Now some pre-work on the Follow-Up Frame

inbodydescription.para: Write the same sequenceId to the Follow-up frame
Format the Timestamp (RTC) into correct byte positioning. Note: this is how the Timestamp is stored in the PTP frame itself (transmitted MSB of Epoch first):
Write the Timestamp (RTC) to the Follow-up frame

briefdescription.para: A function to format then request the transmission of a PTP Follow-Up Packet.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAvb instance to be worked on

simplesect.para: None. But the relevant Tx PTP Packet Buffer is written to with the updated PTP fields, and then the Tx PTP Packet Buffer Control Register is written to request the frame transmission.

inbodydescription.para: Wait until there are no PTP frames to be transmitted
Read the current RTC offset
Read the Timestamp and adjust it for the MAC transmit latency
Adjust the Timestamp with current RTC ns offset
Calculate the Correction Field
Format the Correction Field into correct byte positioning for PTP frame storage in the buffer
Write the Correction Field to the Follow Up frame
Send the Follow Up Frame!

briefdescription.para: A function to format then request the transmission of a PTP PDelay Request Packet.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAvb instance to be worked on

simplesect.para: None. But the relevant Tx PTP Packet Buffer is written to with the updated PTP fields, and then the Tx PTP Packet Buffer Control Register is written to request the frame transmission.

inbodydescription.para: Wait until there are no PTP frames to be transmitted
Increment the SequenceId
Send the PDelayReq Frame!
Wait for the frame to be transmitted
Capture the Timestamp for Tx of PDelayReq (t1) and adjust it for MAC transmit latency
Capture the SequenceID of the the PDelayReq

briefdescription.para: A function to format then request the transmission of a PTP PDelay Response Packet.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAvb instance to be worked on
is the base address of the received Announce Packet in the Rx PTP Packet Buffer

simplesect.para: None. But the relevant Tx PTP Packet Buffer is written to with the updated PTP fields, and then the Tx PTP Packet Buffer Control Register is written to request the frame transmission.

inbodydescription.para: Wait until there are no PTP frames to be transmitted
Capture the current Synchronised time
Read the current RTC offset
Read the TimestampT2 for PDelayReq reception and adjust it for MAC receive latency
The TimestampT2 was captured using syntonised ns time. We need to convert this into synchronised time by adding on the current offset
Check for ns wrap-around condition

Even though we read the RTC value at the beginning of this function, there would have been processing delay between the actual reception (and timestamping) of the PDelayReq frame and the start of this function. During this time, the RTC Seconds field could have wrapped around. We need to detect this and if it has done, the slave Seconds field would also have incremented (so it needs to be set back).
NanoSec has wrapped since timestamp was taken so decrement the Seconds field
Format the Timestamp (t2) into correct byte positioning for PTP frame storage, then write the Timestamp (t2) to the PDelayResp frame
Set the SequenceId in the PDelayResp and PDelayRespFollowUp frame to be that of the received PDelayReq frame
Copy the sourcePortIdentity field from the PDelayReq into the PDelayResp and PDelayRespFollowUp frame

briefdescription.para: A function to format then request the transmission of a PTP PDelay Response Follow-Up Packet.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAvb instance to be worked on

simplesect.para: None. But the relevant Tx PTP Packet Buffer is written to with the updated PTP fields, and then the Tx PTP Packet Buffer Control Register is written to request the frame transmission.

inbodydescription.para: Wait until there are no PTP frames to be transmitted
Capture the current Synchronised time
Read the TimestampT3 for PDelayResp transmission and adjust it for MAC transmit latency

The TimestampT3 was captured using syntonised ns time. We need to convert this into synchronised time by adding on the ns offset. We use the same offset here as for the PDelayResp frame since if a ns offset change had been made between PDelayResp and , PDelayRespFollowUp, this would result in an error in the link delay measurement.
Check for ns wrap-around condition

Even though we read the RTC value at the beginning of this function, there would have been processing delay between the actual reception (and timestamping) of the PDelayReq frame and the start of this function. During this time, the RTC seconds field could have wrapped around. We need to detect this and if it has done, the slave seconds field would also have incremented (so it needs to be set back).
nanosec has wrapped since timestamp was taken so decrement the seconds field
Format the Timestamp (t3) into correct byte positioning for PTP frame storage, the write the Timestamp (t3) to the PDelayRespFollowUp frame

briefdescription.para: A function to check that various fields in the received frame contain the expected values which define it as a valid AVB PTP frame.

detaileddescription.para: If this check does not pass then the frame should not be decoded and used.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAvb instance to be worked on
is the base address of the received Announce Packet in the Rx PTP Packet Buffer

simplesect.para: An updated True/False decision as to whether this received frame really is a valid PTP type.

inbodydescription.para: Start by assuming that it is a valid PTP frame
Perform a 32-bit read from the relevant position in the frame
Check the Length/Type field for a valid Ethertype
Check the versionPTP

briefdescription.para: A function to decode a received PTP Sync Packet.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAvb instance to be worked on
is the base address of the received Announce Packet in the Rx PTP Packet Buffer

inbodydescription.para: Read sourcePortIdentity from packet
Only decode if configured for a slave and if SourcePortID is that of the RTC Clock Master
Reset Sync Interval Counter as we have received a valid Sync
Capture the local Timestamp for receipt of this frame and adjust it for MAC receive latency
Capture the Sync SequenceID
Capture the logMeanMessageInterval and convert into a useful duration (NOTE: there is an implicit conversion from u32 to char here)
We don't need to capture the correction field - unless we want to check that it is 0.

briefdescription.para: A function to decode a received PTP Follow-up Packet.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAvb instance to be worked on
is the base address of the received Announce Packet in the Rx PTP Packet Buffer

inbodydescription.para: Read sourcePortIdentity from packet
Only decode if configured for a slave and if SA is that of the RTC Clock Master
Capture the Follow Up SequenceID
SequenceID in Follow Up Frame should always match that of the Sync Frame
Capture the correction field from follow up frame
Perform the Course RTC Offset correction for every Sync / FollowUp pair
Every n Sync / FollowUp pairs, we are going to calculate a corrected increment rate of RTC
Reset the CounterSyncEvents Counter
Capture the Sequence ID of the Follow Up frame
Perform the RTC increment rate adjustment calculation
Sample Sync Frame Time sent (as estimated by the slave) for comparison in ten more repetition's time
Sample Sync Frame Time sent (as calculated by the master) for comparison in ten more repetition's time
Sample the current Follow Up Sequence ID for comparison in ten more repetition's time

briefdescription.para: A function to decode a received PDelayResp Packet.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAvb instance to be worked on
is the base address of the received Announce Packet in the Rx PTP Packet Buffer

inbodydescription.para: Have we already seen a PDelayResp since the last PDelayReq was sent? If so, ignore the packet
Find the ClockIdentity of the Sender
Is the PDelayResp message from ourself? If so, the Peer is most likely a dumb hub and should be considered not ASCapable
Capture the requestingPortIdentity
Capture the PDelayResp SequenceID
Verify that the requestingPortIdentity matches our portIdentity
Only process if the received frame's sequenceId matches the sequenceId sent in the last pDelay_Req packet
Mark this as a valid PDelayResp packet
Capture timestamp for receipt time of PDelayReq at Master (t2)
Capture timestamp for receipt time of PDelayResp at Slave (t4) and adjust it for MAC receive latency

briefdescription.para: A function to decode a received PDelayRespFollowUp Packet.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAvb instance to be worked on
is the base address of the received Announce Packet in the Rx PTP Packet Buffer

inbodydescription.para: Has a valid PDelayResp packet been received since the last PDelayReq packet was sent?
Capture the PDelayRespFollowUp SequenceID
Get the sourcePortIdentity of the sender
The sourcePortIdentity of the PDelayRespFollowUp should match that of the last PDelayResp packet received
Get the requestingPortIdentity of the sender
The requestingPortIdentity of the PDelayRespFollowUp should match that of the last PDelayResp packet received
SequenceID of PDelayRespFollowUp Frame should always match that of the PDelayResp Frame and the original PDelayReq Frame.
Mark this as a valid PDelayRespFollowUp packet
Capture the timestamp for transmit time of PDelayResp at Master (t3)
Now we know t1, t2, t3 and t4, calculate the link delay

briefdescription.para: A function to decode a received Signalling Packet and modify the TX PTP Buffers based on the requested values.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAvb instance to be worked on
is the base address of the received Signaling Packet in the Rx PTP Packet Buffer

inbodydescription.para: Read the requested logMeanMessage durations from the Signalling frame
update linkDelayInterval
don't change the interval
currently only support the default value
set the interval to initial value
Update logMeanMessageInterval in the pre-loaded TX PDELAYREQ message buffer
stop sending pDelay messages
update timeSyncInterval
don't change the interval
set the interval to initial value
Update logMeanMessageInterval in the pre-loaded TX SYNC message buffer
Update logMeanMessageInterval in the pre-loaded TX FOLLOW_UP message buffer
stop sending sync messages
update announceInterval
don't change the interval
set the interval to initial value
Update logMeanMessageInterval in the pre-loaded TX ANNOUNCE message buffer
stop sending Announce messages

briefdescription.para: A function to update a PTP message Interval Duration (defined as a fraction of 128 seconds).

detaileddescription.para: If the endpoint cannot support a requested logMeanVal then do not perform the conversion - return the current value.

parameterdescription.para: is the Interval Duration to be updated
is the base2 value that is to be converted

simplesect.para: logMeanVal represented as a fraction of 128
This endpoint only supports logMeanValues >=-7 and <=8.

briefdescription.para: A function to convert a logMean (power of 2) value into a fraction of 128 that is compatible with Signalling data.

parameterdescription.para: is the base2 value that is to be converted

simplesect.para: logMeanVal represented as a fraction of 128

briefdescription.para: A function to convert a fraction of 128 value that is compatible with Signalling data into a logMean (power of 2) value;.

parameterdescription.para: is the Signalling data value that is to be converted

simplesect.para: fractionalVal represented as logMean (power of 2) value

inbodydescription.para: just in case fractionalVal is not a power of 2, we'll only look at the most significant bit Count how many shifts it takes for most significant set bit to be in the highest (16th) bit location
logMeanVal = 0 = 2^0 = 128/128 would give us a numShifts result of 8, so 8 will be our base

briefdescription.para: A function to update the logMeanMessageInterval field in a PTP packet.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the device
is the base address of the TX PTP Buffer to be updated
is the "fraction of 128" value of the data to be written

simplesect.para: None. But the relevant Tx PTP Packet Buffer is written to with the updated logMeanMessageInterval

inbodydescription.para: Convert intervalDuration to a logMean value
Read the current fields
Update just the logMeanMessageInterval field
Write back

briefdescription.para: This function updates the portIdLocal local copy of the sourcePortIdentity and writes this value into the TX PTP frame buffer templates.

detaileddescription.para: The fields that are written are: o sourcePortIdentity for all default PTP frames o Announce:: grandmasterIdentity o Announce:: TLV clockIdentity

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAvb instance to be worked on
is the clockIdentity and portNumber for this endpoint

simplesect.para: Announce::TLV. By default the tlvType and length field are set up in the BRAM, assuming that N = 1.

inbodydescription.para: Write the sourcePortIdentity into the header for all TX PTP buffers except the empty default buffer AND write the GMID for TX announce AND Write the ClockIdentity into the TX Announce TLV

(a) Write the upper 2 bytes of the ClockIdentityUpper
REM: Swap back the byte order into frame storage order

(b) Write the lower 2 bytes of the ClockIdentityUpper and upper 2 bytes of the ClockIdentityLower.
REM: Swap back the byte order into frame storage order

(c) Write the lower 2 bytes of the ClockIdentityLower and the portNumber
REM: Swap back the byte order into frame storage order
Write the grandmasterIdentity into the header for the TX Announce PTP buffer
(a) Write 1 byte of GMID (Upper)
(b) Write 3 bytes of GMID (Upper) and 1 byte of GMID (Lower)
(c) Write 3 bytes of GMID (Lower)

briefdescription.para: The XAvb driver.

detaileddescription.para: Functions in this file all contain functions which decode the received Precise Timing Protocol (PTP) frames, or to format and transmit PTP frames.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a mbr 09/19/08 First release 1.01a mbr 06/24/09 PTP frame format updates for IEEE802.1 AS draft 5-0 2_02a mbr 09/16/09 Updates for programmable PTP timers 2_04a kag 07/23/10 PTP frame format updates for IEEE802.1 AS draft 6-7 3_01a kag 08/29/11 Added new APIs to update the RX Filter Control Reg. Fix for CR:572539. Updated bit map for Rx Filter control reg.
